appointment_information.department_division: Department of Medicine, Division of Hematology/Oncology
appointment_information.scientific_interest_area_of_research: Translational research in hematologic malignancies, with a particular focus on minimal residual disease (MRD) detection and risk stratification in acute myeloid leukemia (AML) and myelodysplastic syndromes (MDS). I am especially interested in integrating next-generation sequencing (NGS)-based assays and multiparameter flow cytometry with clinical data to better predict relapse and guide post-remission therapy, including allogeneic stem cell transplantation and maintenance strategies. My prior work has involved developing and validating custom NGS panels for somatic mutation profiling, analyzing clonal evolution under selective pressure from targeted therapies, and exploring mechanisms of treatment resistance. Over the course of this fellowship, I hope to (1) refine MRD detection thresholds that are clinically actionable, (2) participate in the design and conduct of investigator-initiated clinical trials incorporating molecular MRD endpoints, and (3) collaborate with biostatisticians and computational biologists to build prognostic models that combine genomic, immunophenotypic, and clinical variables. Long term, I aim to establish an independent research program that bridges laboratory discovery and patient care, ultimately improving outcomes for adults with high-risk myeloid neoplasms.
